title: TC-DFU-001: Verify 'Update All' DFU failure when headset connected to Poly Studio Mobile app during PC downgrade — no retry option
steps: 1. Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds to the Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Ensure the earbuds are also connected to a PC via Bluetooth.
3. On the PC, initiate a firmware downgrade for the earbuds using Poly Lens Desktop or Plantronics Hub.
4. While the PC downgrade is in progress, open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
5. Tap 'Update All' to start a firmware update from the app.
6. Observe the DFU process on the app — note any error message or failure indication.
7. After the failure, check if a 'Retry' button or any retry option is displayed on the Firmware Update screen.
8. Close and reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app.
9. Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update again.
expected: The 'Update All' DFU fails with an error message. No 'Retry' button or retry option is shown on the Firmware Update screen. After app restart, the firmware update status remains stale (e.g., still shows 'Update Available' or 'Update All' even though the update failed or was interrupted).

title: TC-DFU-002: Verify stale update status persists after app restart when DFU fails during PC downgrade
steps: 1. Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Connect the same device to a PC via Bluetooth.
3. On the PC, start a firmware downgrade using Poly Lens Desktop.
4. During the PC downgrade, open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
5. Tap 'Update All' and allow the DFU to fail (due to concurrent PC downgrade).
6. Note the firmware version shown on the app after failure.
7. Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recent apps).
8. Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app and reconnect to the device.
9. Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update.
10. Compare the displayed firmware version and update status with the version noted in step 6.
expected: The firmware update status remains stale after app restart — the app still shows the same outdated firmware version and 'Update Available' or 'Update All' prompt, even though the device may have been partially updated or is in an inconsistent state. No indication of the previous failed update attempt is shown.

title: TC-DFU-003: Verify 'Update All' DFU failure when PC downgrade completes before app DFU starts
steps: 1. Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Connect the same device to a PC via Bluetooth.
3. On the PC, complete a full firmware downgrade using Poly Lens Desktop.
4. Without disconnecting from PC, open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
5. Tap 'Update All' to start the DFU from the app.
6. Observe the DFU process for any error or failure.
7. After failure, check for a 'Retry' button on the Firmware Update screen.
8. Close and reopen the app, then navigate back to Firmware Update.
expected: The 'Update All' DFU fails or behaves unexpectedly (e.g., shows incorrect version, hangs, or errors out). No retry option is available. After app restart, the update status is stale and does not reflect the actual firmware version on the device.

title: TC-DFU-004: Verify 'Update All' DFU failure when PC downgrade is initiated during app DFU transfer phase
steps: 1. Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Connect the same device to a PC via Bluetooth.
3. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
4. Tap 'Update All' and wait for the DFU transfer to begin (progress bar appears).
5. While the app DFU transfer is in progress (e.g., at 30-50%), start a firmware downgrade on the PC using Poly Lens Desktop.
6. Observe the app DFU progress — note any error, freeze, or crash.
7. After the app DFU fails, check if a 'Retry' button is displayed.
8. Force-kill and reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app.
9. Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update.
expected: The app DFU fails during the transfer phase when the PC downgrade is initiated. No retry option is shown. After app restart, the firmware update status is stale — the app may still show the old firmware version and 'Update Available' even though the device firmware state is now inconsistent due to the interrupted dual update.

title: TC-DFU-005: Verify no retry option after 'Update All' DFU failure due to concurrent PC downgrade — device reconnection scenario
steps: 1. Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Connect the same device to a PC via Bluetooth.
3. On the PC, start a firmware downgrade using Poly Lens Desktop.
4. During the PC downgrade, open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
5. Tap 'Update All' and let the DFU fail.
6. After failure, disconnect the device from the PC Bluetooth.
7. On the Poly Studio Mobile app, check if a 'Retry' button appears on the Firmware Update screen.
8. Tap the device name to go back to Device Settings, then re-enter Firmware Update.
9. Disconnect and reconnect the earbuds from the phone Bluetooth (Phone Settings → Bluetooth → Forget device, then re-pair).
10. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app, navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update.
expected: No 'Retry' button appears on the Firmware Update screen even after disconnecting from PC. Navigating away and back does not show a retry option. After Bluetooth re-pairing and app reconnection, the firmware update status remains stale — the app does not recover to show the correct firmware version or offer a fresh update option.

title: TC-DFU-006: Verify 'Update All' DFU failure leaves device in inconsistent state — audio functionality check
steps: 1. Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Connect the same device to a PC via Bluetooth.
3. On the PC, start a firmware downgrade using Poly Lens Desktop.
4. During the PC downgrade, open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Firmware Update.
5. Tap 'Update All' and allow the DFU to fail.
6. After the failure, close the Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Play audio media (music or video) on the phone.
8. Make a phone call using the earbuds.
9. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment.
10. Adjust the Surround level slider and verify audio pass-through.
11. Switch to Speech sub-setting and adjust the Free level slider.
12. Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update and check the status.
expected: Audio playback and call functionality may be degraded or non-functional after the failed DFU. Transparency Mode settings (Surround and Free levels) may not persist or may behave incorrectly. The Firmware Update screen shows a stale status with no retry option, and the device firmware version may be incorrect or blank.
